meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Build Your Portfolio as a Student
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing to build a standout portfolio. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first project and land your dream dev role today.
content.answer_block: Transform your lack of experience into a compelling case study. By implementing a sophisticated SaaS pricing model, you demonstrate commercial awareness and full-stack technical depth. This guide bridges the gap between 'student project' and 'market-ready SaaS,' providing the exact architectural blueprints needed to showcase your engineering prowess to future employers.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank screen of your GitHub profile, the silence of 'zero commits' echoing the uncertainty of your career prospects. Every job posting demands experience you haven't been given the chance to earn, creating a paradox where you need a job to get the experience, and experience to get the job. It is a grueling cycle that breaks most aspiring developers.

But the rules have changed. Today, the most valuable currency isn't 'years of experience'—it's the ability to translate complex logic into a profitable, user-facing product. By building a SaaS pricing engine, you aren't just writing code; you are architecting a business. This is your path to escaping the 'no portfolio' trap and proving you can ship real-world value.

steps.title: Define Your Value Tiers
steps.description: Establish a three-tier pricing structure (Free, Pro, Enterprise) to demonstrate your understanding of market segmentation and user psychology.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a data schema for a SaaS pricing model with three distinct tiers, including feature flags, monthly vs annual billing logic, and Stripe integration endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize pricing schema and Stripe checkout session logic'

steps.title: Build the Dynamic Pricing Engine
steps.description: Develop a logic layer that calculates subscription costs based on usage metrics, a critical skill for modern AI-driven SaaS applications.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust pricing engine function that calculates prorated subscription costs based on usage-based metrics like tokens consumed or API calls made.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/pricing_engine.ts --instruction 'Implement usage-based billing calculation logic'

steps.title: Integrate Secure Payment Flows
steps.description: Connect your frontend to Stripe API to handle secure payment processing, ensuring you understand PCI compliance and webhook handling.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Express.js webhook handler for Stripe events, specifically focusing on subscription.created and invoice.payment_succeeded.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Configure Stripe webhook listener and database user upgrade trigger'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Build middleware that checks the user's subscription status before granting access to premium API endpoints or UI components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an authentication middleware that validates user subscription status against a database record before allowing access to restricted API routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/middleware/auth.ts --instruction 'Add subscription status check for premium features'

steps.title: Showcase Your Metrics Dashboard
steps.description: Create an analytics view for the user, showing their current spend, usage, and tier status, demonstrating frontend visualization skills.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component that visualizes user usage metrics compared to their current plan limits using Recharts or similar libraries.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Build user usage analytics dashboard component'

pro_tips: Always implement 'graceful degradation' for expired subscriptions so users aren't locked out instantly.
Use database transactions for subscription status updates to avoid race conditions during payment processing.
Hard-code your feature flags in a config file first, then move them to your database for dynamic control.
Include an 'Admin Dashboard' view in your portfolio to show you understand how to manage users, not just build features for them.
Always document your API error handling for payment failures; employers value defensive programming over raw speed.
content.next_steps: Deploy your application to Vercel or Railway, add a 'Case Study' README section explaining your technical decisions, and share your live demo on LinkedIn to attract recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. You have the tools, the architecture, and the strategy to build a portfolio that commands attention. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
